title: symphytum brachycalyx
intro: Symphytum brachycalyx, also known as small leaved comfrey, is a perennial herb that belongs to the borage family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and wound-healing effects, due to the presence of allantoin, a compound that promotes tissue repair and regeneration. The health benefits of Symphytum brachycalyx include reducing joint pain and swelling, as well as promoting bone growth and healing. It is typically prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve, and is often used to treat sprains, strains, and other musculoskeletal injuries.
